tweets.text:
One of the main issues for China buying crude is the elevated freight cost. 

Now that a chunk of tankers that were stuck have now exited, it will be interesting to see what happens to tanker rates. 

At the moment, 10 tankers are exiting using the Iran route. Miracle Hope, which previously tried the Oman route just abruptly went north. 

Only time will tell what’s going on.

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

US tanker escort is back with a large group of tankers going out and coming in.

We tracked 3 VLCCs inbound ending with "prosperity". If you look at the VLCCs waiting in Fujairah, there will be another 3 to 4 Prosperity VLCCs going in.

The issue I'm seeing is that there aren't enough tankers to even do the ship-to-ship transfers at the speed needed to fully reverse the production shut-in.

Iran is also short on VLCCs, so it will use some of Iraq's loading to export its crude.

Flow rate is still insufficient, so while the oil market is contending with producers dumping barrels from floating storage and onshore storage, we need a much larger backlog to alleviate the real problem: production shut-in.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and included the table of inbound tankers.
tweets.full_text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

US tanker escort is back with a large group of tankers going out and coming in.

We tracked 3 VLCCs inbound ending with "prosperity". If you look at the VLCCs waiting in Fujairah, there will be another 3 to 4 Prosperity VLCCs going in.

The issue I'm seeing is that there aren't enough tankers to even do the ship-to-ship transfers at the speed needed to fully reverse the production shut-in.

Iran is also short on VLCCs, so it will use some of Iraq's loading to export its crude.

Flow rate is still insufficient, so while the oil market is contending with producers dumping barrels from floating storage and onshore storage, we need a much larger backlog to alleviate the real problem: production shut-in.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and included the table of inbound tankers.

tweets.text:
It seems like Trump started a new era in politics.  The TV era that started with JFK required general good looking charismatic leaders.

Now we’re seeing the rise of politicians that know how to use social media impecably.  Bonus points if they have previous public feuds, so they know how to handle what might otherwise be career ending surprises.

Spencer Pratt, Hunter Biden, Zohran Mamdani, David Portnoy (just teased running for mayor this week) all fit this mold pretty perfectly.   Expect to see this trend accelerate.
